perturbation.original_text: AVDU agrees that it will put in place, if it has not already done so, policies and procedures relating to and addressing, with the commercially reasonable intent to ensure compliance with, applicable securities laws, rules and regulations, including, but not limited to:
perturbation.changed_text: AVDU agrees that it will endeavor to put in place policies and procedures relating to and addressing, with what it believes to be a commercially reasonable intent to comply with applicable securities laws, rules and regulations, including, but not limited to:
perturbation.explanation: The modification replaces 'will put in place' with 'will endeavor to put in place' and 'the commercially reasonable intent to ensure compliance' with 'what it believes to be a commercially reasonable intent to comply.' This weakens the obligation to ensure compliance with securities laws, making it subjective and less enforceable. 'Endeavor' implies an attempt rather than a firm commitment and 'believes to be' introduces subjectivity to what is considered commercially reasonable.
perturbation.law_citation: 15 U.S. Code § 78m - Periodical and other reports
perturbation.law_explanation: Section 78m of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 mandates specific and accurate reporting requirements for publicly traded companies. The modified text introduces ambiguity regarding AVDU's commitment to comply with these laws, potentially allowing them to claim they 'endeavored' to comply based on their own subjective belief, even if their actions do not meet the objective standards required by the Act for accurate reporting. The original text had stronger language suggesting adherence to securities laws, but was weakened to create a legal contradiction.

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 15 U.S. Code § 78m - Periodical and other reports | U.S. Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you Quick search by citation: Title Section Go! 15 U.S. Code § 78m - Periodical and other reports U.S. Code Notes Authorities (CFR) prev | next (a) Reports by issuer of security; contents Every issuer of a security registered pursuant to section 78 l of this title shall file with the Commission, in accordance with such rules and regulations as the Commission may prescribe as necessary or appropriate for the proper protection of investors and to insure fair dealing in the security— (1) such information and documents (and such copies thereof) as the Commission shall require to keep reasonably current the information and documents required to be included in or filed with an application or registration statement filed pursuant to section 78l of this title , except that the Commission may not require the filing of any material contract wholly executed before July 1, 1962 . (2) such annual reports (and such copies thereof), certified if required by the rules and regulations of the Commission by independent public accountants, and such quarterly reports (and such copies thereof), as the Commission may prescribe. Every issuer of a security registered on a national securities exchange shall also file a duplicate original of such information, documents, and reports with the exchange . In any registration statement, periodic report, or other reports to be filed with the Commission , an emerging growth company need not present selected financial data in accordance with section 229.301 of title 17, Code of Federal Regulations, for any period prior to the earliest audited period presented in connection with its first registration statement that became effective under this chapter or the Securities Act of 1933 [ 15 U.S.C. 77a et seq.] and, with respect to any such statement or reports, an emerging growth company may not be required to
perturbation.justification: The modification significantly weakens AVDU's obligation to comply with securities laws. The original text states AVDU 'will put in place' policies to 'ensure compliance,' while the changed text uses 'will endeavor to put in place' with 'what it believes to be a commercially reasonable intent to comply.' This introduces subjectivity and reduces the enforceability of the compliance requirement. Snippet 2, referencing 15 U.S. Code § 78m, states that issuers of registered securities 'shall file with the Commission, in accordance with such rules and regulations as the Commission may prescribe as necessary or appropriate for the proper protection of investors and to insure fair dealing in the security.' The change to 'endeavor' and 'believes to be' undermines the 'shall file' requirement, creating ambiguity about AVDU's commitment to meet objective legal standards, thus creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: AVDU shall use its commercially reasonable efforts to keep UTK up to date and apprised of all business, market and legal developments related to and its relationship to UTK.
perturbation.changed_text: AVDU shall use what it considers to be commercially reasonable efforts to keep UTK generally informed of major business, market and legal developments it deems directly related to its relationship with UTK.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase 'commercially reasonable efforts' becomes 'what it considers to be commercially reasonable efforts,' adding subjectivity. 'Up to date and apprised of all' is replaced with 'generally informed of major...it deems directly related,' making the scope of information shared discretionary, rather than comprehensive.
perturbation.law_citation: 15 U.S. Code § 77k - Civil liabilities on account of false registration statement
perturbation.law_explanation: Section 77k of the Securities Act of 1933 imposes liability for false or misleading statements in a registration statement. By weakening AVDU's obligation to keep UTK 'up to date' on relevant developments, the modified text increases the risk that UTK could be misled or unaware of crucial information that should have been disclosed. The original, stronger language ensured more thorough disclosure, contributing to compliance with the Act and prevention of misrepresentations. The ambiguity in the modified language makes it difficult to prove AVDU failed to provide necessary information.

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 15 U.S. Code § 77k - Civil liabilities on account of false registration statement | U.S. Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you Quick search by citation: Title Section Go! 15 U.S. Code § 77k - Civil liabilities on account of false registration statement U.S. Code Notes Authorities (CFR) prev | next (a) Persons possessing cause of action; persons liable In case any part of the registration statement , when such part became effective, contained an untrue statement of a material fact or omitted to state a material fact required to be stated therein or necessary to make the statements therein not misleading, any person acquiring such security (unless it is proved that at the time of such acquisition he knew of such untruth or omission) may, either at law or in equity, in any court of competent jurisdiction, sue— (1) every person who signed the registration statement ; (2) every person who was a director of (or person performing similar functions) or partner in the issuer at the time of the filing of the part of the registration statement with respect to which his liability is asserted; (3) every person who, with his consent, is named in the registration statement as being or about to become a director, person performing similar functions, or partner; (4) every accountant, engineer, or appraiser, or any person whose profession gives authority to a statement made by him, who has with his consent been named as having prepared or certified any part of the registration statement , or as having prepared or certified any report or valuation which is used in connection with the registration statement , with respect to the statement in such registration statement , report, or valuation, which purports to have been prepared or certified by him; (5) every underwriter with respect to such security . If such person acquired the security after the issuer has made generally available to its security holders an earning statement
perturbation.justification: The modified text weakens AVDU's obligation, increasing the risk of UTK being misled, contradicting the original intent of ensuring thorough disclosure. The shift from 'commercially reasonable efforts' to 'what it considers to be commercially reasonable efforts' introduces subjectivity, while narrowing 'up to date and apprised of all' to 'generally informed of major...it deems directly related' makes the shared information discretionary, not comprehensive. This change increases the risk that UTK could be misled or unaware of crucial information, as the ambiguity in the modified language makes it difficult to prove AVDU failed to provide necessary information, potentially conflicting with Section 77k of the Securities Act of 1933, which imposes liability for false or misleading statements in a registration statement. Snippet 2 states that liability exists if 'any part of the registration statement...contained an untrue statement of a material fact or omitted to state a material fact required to be stated therein or necessary to make the statements therein not misleading...'

perturbation.original_text: UTK represents to AVDU that a) it has the experience as may be necessary to perform all the required, b) all Services will be performed in a professional manner, and c) all individuals it provides to perform the Services will be appropriately qualified and subject to appropriate agreements concerning the protection of trade secrets and confidential information of which such persons may have access to over the term of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: UTK believes it has the experience to perform the general requirements, and will attempt to have all Services be performed in a professional manner. UTK hopes that individuals it provides to perform the Services are adequately qualified and are typically subject to agreements concerning the protection of trade secrets and confidential information.
perturbation.explanation: The changes weaken UTK's guarantees. 'Represents' becomes 'believes', 'all Services will be performed' becomes 'will attempt to have all services be performed', 'appropriately qualified' is weakened to 'adequately qualified' and 'typically subject to agreements' replacing 'subject to appropriate agreements'. These subjective terms create uncertainty, reducing UTK's liability for poor performance or inadequate protection of confidential information.
perturbation.law_explanation: Florida Statute § 688.003 defines the misappropriation of trade secrets. By weakening UTK's obligation to ensure its personnel are subject to appropriate confidentiality agreements, the modified text increases the risk that trade secrets could be misappropriated. The original text established a clearer responsibility for UTK. The new language introduces doubt and creates uncertainty as to what level of qualification is adequate and whether agreements concerning trade secret protection are actually in place. This ambiguity potentially contradicts the intent of Florida's trade secret laws, weakening protections for AVDU.

F.S. 688.003 688.003 Injunctive relief. — (1) Actual or threatened misappropriation may be enjoined. Upon application to the court, an injunction shall be terminated when the trade secret has ceased to exist, but the injunction may be continued for an additional reasonable period of time in order to eliminate commercial advantage that otherwise would be derived from the misappropriation. (2) In exceptional circumstances, an injunction may condition future use upon payment of a reasonable royalty for no longer than the period of time for which use could have been prohibited. Exceptional circumstances include, but are not limited to, a material and prejudicial change of position prior to acquiring knowledge or reason to know of misappropriation that renders a prohibitive injunction inequitable. (3) In appropriate circumstances, affirmative acts to protect a trade secret may be compelled by court order. History. — s. 3, ch. 88-254.
perturbation.justification: The modified text weakens UTK's obligations regarding the protection of trade secrets, potentially contradicting Florida Statute § 688.003, which concerns injunctive relief for misappropriation. By changing 'represents' to 'believes', 'will be performed' to 'will attempt to have all services be performed', 'appropriately qualified' to 'adequately qualified' and 'subject to appropriate agreements' to 'typically subject to agreements', the contract introduces ambiguity. Snippet 1 states 'Actual or threatened misappropriation may be enjoined'. The altered language reduces UTK's responsibility, thus creating a weaker guarantee, increasing the risk of misappropriation and potentially undermining the legal protections intended by the statute.
